narrative:
A cold front is crossing the mountains this morning, west to east, and Arthur's remnant is scooting quickly off to the east from Upstate SC. Showers end early for most of us but linger a bit right along the western escarpment. We see partial sunshine today, cooler and breezy. After a crisp start Saturday, we enjoy a pleasant afternoon. Sunday turns a bit warmer, and I can't rule out a stray afternoon shower. The next front arrives Monday evening from the northwest, with showers and thunderstorms developing west to east late morning and tapering off after the evening.

This week's "Where in the Weather is Ray?" covers rain from the remnant of Tropical Storm Arthur. The featured location is Wolf Laurel Resort.

Breezy, Cooler, Improving

Last Updated Fri June 19 at 10:59AM by Ray Russell

A cold front is crossing the mountains this morning, west to east, and Arthur's remnant is scooting quickly off to the east from Upstate SC. Showers end early for most of us but linger a bit right along the western escarpment. We see partial sunshine today, cooler and breezy. After a crisp start Saturday, we enjoy a pleasant afternoon. Sunday turns a bit warmer, and I can't rule out a stray afternoon shower. The next front arrives Monday evening from the northwest, with showers and thunderstorms developing west to east late morning and tapering off after the evening.

This week's "Where in the Weather is Ray?" covers rain from the remnant of Tropical Storm Arthur. The featured location is Wolf Laurel Resort.

Forecast Discussion

Getting the Arthur mess out of the way solidifies the next several days' forecast and makes our job much easier this morning. A front passes through from the west this morning. The remnant of Arthur heads quickly ENE from Upstate SC.

Showers end for most of the region early this morning; however, a few linger along the western escarpment in a cool, breezy NW flow. Partial sun develops later this morning and this afternoon, but not "clearing." Cooler air digs in, making tonight and early Saturday chilly.

High pressure moves from the Ohio Valley to the Southern Appalachians this weekend. After that crisp start Saturday, the afternoon is as good as it gets. Sunday afternoon is slightly warmer, with the "barely worth mentioning" potential for a stray shower or thundershower.

The next front from the northwest arrives Monday evening. That front stalls to our south. Clouds increase Sunday night. Showers and thunderstorms develop west to east late morning. Expect showers and thunderstorms in the afternoon and early evening, before tapering off overnight.

Tuesday is cooler again. I cannot rule out a stray shower Tuesday. Then, with the front to our south and another approaching from the north, temperatures slowly warm Wednesday and Thursday. Both days carry the possibility of an isolated afternoon shower or thundershower.

Detailed forecast discussion and analysis is only available to RaysWeather supporters.

Jun 18, 2026
Monday Message for June 15, 2026

It's the pre-summer solstice edition of the Monday Message, available for subscribers. El Niño has officially arrived, and will have significant implications for our weather heading into fall and winter.

Jun 15, 2026
El Niño Has Officially Arrived

El Niño has officially arrived, as described in the latest Climate Prediction Center ENSO discussion.

This will have significant implications on fall and winter temperature and precipitation in the Southeastern United States. While our region continues to experience significant drought conditions, above-normal precipitation will ultimately make a comeback during fall and winter.
